other_observers: Larry Allison; At this location...they were reported elsewhere on the Western Slope in December.  Ron Lambeth put a photo on WSBN in mid-December of a redpoll in his yard in the redlands.
duration: 1 1/2 hours we spent with the flock
specific_location: 2-4 miles (we were in the flock continuously over this distance) - west from the V road intersection with highway 330
description: 1. Birds were in a very rural area within large (hundreds) of rosy finches of all three types.  2. Size, yellow bill, shape of bill, coloration pattern were diagnostic.Behaviors: You'll note there are three in one of the photos, I attached. At least once, in a different location, I had six redpolls in the same binocular view. I took over 60 photographs in the hour or so we spent with these birds. I have photos where all of the birds besides the redpolls were black rosy-finches and others where they were all gray-crowned.Call: Within large flock of rosy finches-call not noted, probably not really discernible in such a large group of birdsPlumage: adult winter
